Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.1 REST Signal
Requirement Summary: REST contributes up to 30 pts of the 100-point Health Score. The top tier (≥12.70 V mean rest voltage) maps to 30 pts (Excellent). If wrong, a healthy battery scores low and triggers false replacement forecasts.
Title: Verify scoring REST signal as 30 pts when 30-day mean rest_voltage_avg is ≥ 12.70 V
Preconditions:
• Vehicle has ≥ 30 days of telemetry in the Flespi tenant
• Calc 2805151 (Rest Voltage) is enabled and producing rest_voltage_avg samples
• 30-day mean rest_voltage_avg is engineered to be 12.72 V
• Test user is logged in to Fleet Pulse and the vehicle row is visible on the dashboard
Steps (Action):
1. Open the Flespi calc 2805151 device messages and confirm at least 20 rest_voltage_avg samples in the last 30 days
2. In Fleet Pulse, open the Health Score tooltip for the vehicle
3. Read the REST score in the tooltip
4. Trigger a manual score recalculation (or wait for the next 15-min cycle) and re-open the tooltip
Expected Result:
1. Sample count ≥ 20 and the visible average reads 12.72 V (±0.01)
2. Tooltip opens and the REST row is visible
3. REST shows 30/30 with the Excellent status indicator (green)
4. REST remains 30/30 — score is stable across recalc
Test Data:
vehicle_id: TLX-DEMO-001
mean_rest_voltage_30d: 12.72 V
sample_count: 24
calc_id: 2805151
Postconditions:
• REST score persisted as 30 in the score_history table
• Overall battery_health_score reflects REST=30 in its breakdown payload

3. REST shows 25/30 with Good status (green)
Postconditions: • REST score persisted as 25 in score_history

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.1 REST Signal
Requirement Summary: When live voltage is missing; the forecast must fall back to mean_rest as its reference voltage (ref_v = live_v if available else mean_rest). Without this; forecast cannot compute.
Title: Verify forecast reference voltage falls back to mean rest voltage when live voltage is missing
Preconditions:
• Vehicle has a valid 30-day mean rest_voltage_avg = 12.40 V
• Latest telemetry message has external.powersource.voltage = null (simulated by removing the last 6 h of msgs)
Steps (Action):
1. Confirm latest message lacks powersource.voltage
2. Trigger forecast recalc
3. Inspect forecast_audit.ref_voltage
4. Open Health Score tooltip
Expected Result:
1. Field is absent in raw payload
2. Forecast pipeline runs without error
3. ref_voltage == 12.40 V (mean rest) not 0 or null
4. LATEST row shows neutral 5/10 and tooltip text 'Live voltage unavailable — using rest mean'

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.2 CRANK Signal — temperature compensation
Requirement Summary: Temperature compensation: crank_compensated = crank_observed + 0.025 × (25 − ambient_temp_c). Required so cold-morning measurements don't false-fail healthy batteries.
Title: Verify CRANK temperature compensation formula adds 0.475 V when raw crank is 9.6 V at 6 °C ambient
Preconditions:
• Ambient temperature for the vehicle's location at the time of the crank event is 6 °C (from WeatherAPI)
• Raw crank_dip captured by calc 2805150 = 9.60 V
Steps (Action):
1. Confirm raw crank_dip = 9.60 V at ambient 6 °C
2. Open forecast_audit.compensated_crank_dip
3. Open tooltip
Expected Result:
1. Both values read correctly
2. Equals 9.60 + 0.025×(25-6) = 10.075 V
3. CRANK = 25/25 (Strong) — would have been 13/25 without compensation

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.2 CRANK Signal — temperature compensation
Requirement Summary: At ambient 25 °C the compensation factor is zero — raw == compensated. Sanity check the formula's baseline.
Title: Verify CRANK temperature compensation adds zero adjustment at the 25 °C baseline
Preconditions: • Raw crank_dip = 9.60 V at ambient 25 °C
Steps (Action):
1. Confirm raw crank_dip and temp
2. Read compensated value
Expected Result:
1. 9.60 V at 25 °C
2. Equals 9.60 V (no adjustment)

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.2 CRANK Signal — REST fallback
Requirement Summary: When no crank events in 30 days; CRANK falls back proportionally to REST: CRANK = round(25 × rest_pts / 30). Validates fallback existence and accuracy.
Title: Verify CRANK signal falls back to round(25 × REST/30) when no crank events exist in 30-day window
Preconditions:
• Vehicle has zero crank events in the 30-day window (e.g. parked in yard)
• REST scored 25/30
Steps (Action):
1. Confirm calc 2805150 returns 0 events
2. Open tooltip and read REST and CRANK rows
3. Repeat with REST=30/30
4. Repeat with REST=0/30

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.2 CRANK Signal — calculator config
Requirement Summary: Regression: calc 2805150 selector MUST use min_active=0 with merge_message_before/after=true. Without this; 99% of crank events are missed (April 28 fix).
Title: Verify calc 2805150 captures cranks correctly with min_active=0 and merge_message_before/after=true
Preconditions: • Vehicle drove ≥ 5 ignition cycles in a test session
Steps (Action):
1. Open calc 2805150 config in Flespi
2. Count ignition OFF→ON transitions in raw telemetry for the test session
3. Count crank events produced by calc 2805150
4. Toggle merge_message_before/after to false and recount
5. Restore configuration to true
Expected Result:
1. min_active=0; merge_message_before=true; merge_message_after=true
2. ≥ 5
3. Matches transition count within ±1
4. Event count drops by ≥ 90% — confirms regression
5. Config is restored
Postconditions: • Calc config matches required values; production capture rate ≥ 95%
Notes: Reverts the April 28 misconfiguration bug.

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.3 TREND Signal — regression x-axis fix
Requirement Summary: Regression: x-axis for slope regression MUST be (interval_begin + interval_end) / 2; NOT the calc timestamp. v2.0 had this wrong and masked BMW battery instability.
Title: Verify TREND regression x-axis uses interval midpoint not the calc emission timestamp
Preconditions:
• Test dataset with 25 rest intervals where interval midpoint differs from calc timestamp by ≥ 30 min
• Reference slope computed via midpoint = −0.011 V/day; using timestamp incorrectly yields −0.004 V/day
Steps (Action):
1. Run TREND regression on the dataset
2. Confirm slope = −0.011 V/day (midpoint method)
3. Inspect the implementation/audit log
4. Open tooltip
Expected Result:
1. Pipeline emits slope value
2. Matches reference within ±0.0005
3. x_axis source field reads 'interval_midpoint'
4. TREND scores 5/12 (matches midpoint slope; not 9/12 which timestamp method would produce)
Postconditions: • TREND audit confirms midpoint method; no v2.0 regression

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.4 DECAY Signal — data cleanup rules
Requirement Summary: Two mandatory data-cleanup rules in DECAY: drop parks shorter than 6 h (noise) AND parks where start voltage > 13.5 V (alternator tail not yet decayed).
Title: Verify DECAY pipeline drops parks shorter than 6 h and parks starting above 13.5 V before computing slope
Preconditions: • Test dataset of 10 parks: 5 valid (≥6 h and start ≤13.5 V); 3 short (4-5 h); 2 with start voltage 13.6-13.9 V
Steps (Action):
1. Run DECAY pipeline on the dataset
2. Inspect intermediate 'qualifying_parks' list
3. Inspect calc 2805232 config
4. Verify slope is computed only over the 5 valid parks
Expected Result:
1. Pipeline completes
2. Count = 5; the 3 short and 2 high-start parks are excluded
3. merge_message_before/after = false (so alternator voltage doesn't contaminate park edges)
4. Audit shows 5 contributing intervals
Test Data:
parks_total: 10
parks_qualifying: 5
parks_dropped_short: 3
parks_dropped_high_v: 2
config.merge_before: false
config.merge_after: false
Postconditions: • Only 5 parks contributed to DECAY slope

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.4 DECAY Signal — slope clamping
Requirement Summary: Negative slope (start < end during a park) is a surface-charge artefact; not real recovery. Slope must be clamped to ≥0.
Title: Verify DECAY slope is clamped to zero when a park shows negative voltage change
Preconditions: • One park where start = 12.30 V; end = 12.35 V over 8 h (artefact)
Steps (Action):
1. Run DECAY pipeline on the artefact park
2. Inspect that park's contribution to slope
3. Confirm no negative slopes propagate into mean
Expected Result:
1. Pipeline completes
2. Raw slope = −0.00625 V/hr; clamped slope = 0.000 V/hr
3. Mean slope uses 0.000 from this park; not the negative value
Postconditions: • DECAY mean computed correctly with clamped values

4. Verify final multiplier
Expected Result:

4. Equals 0.5 × 0.5 = 0.25 (rest kept over deep) — base × 0.25
Test Data:

expected_post_guard: crank<9.5; rest<11.8 (deep dropped)
expected_multiplier: 0.25

3. Only friendly labels and dot colours present
Postconditions: • No forbidden tokens present in tooltip

Screen / Section: Fleet Pulse: Health Score Tooltip Spec — Band label colour
Requirement Summary: Band label inside the tooltip is colour-coded per band hex (Excellent #1B873F; Good #2E7D32; Fair #F4A300; Poor #E76F00; Critical #C0392B).
Title: Verify Health Score tooltip band label uses the documented hex colour for each of the 5 bands
Preconditions: • 5 seeded vehicles; one per band
Steps (Action):
1. Open tooltip on Excellent vehicle
2. Open tooltip on Good vehicle
3. Open tooltip on Fair vehicle
4. Open tooltip on Poor vehicle
5. Open tooltip on Critical vehicle
Postconditions: • All 5 band labels render the correct hex

Screen / Section: Fleet Pulse: Health Score Tooltip Spec — Mobile modal
Requirement Summary: On viewports < 480 px the tooltip must open as a tap-to-open modal sheet rather than an inline tooltip. Tap-anywhere-to-close dismisses.
Title: Verify Health Score tooltip opens as a modal sheet on tap when viewport is narrower than 480 px
Preconditions: • Fleet Pulse open on mobile viewport 479 px wide
Steps (Action):
1. Tap the Health Score chip
2. Tap outside the modal
3. Resize to 480 px and tap again
Expected Result:
1. Modal sheet opens (full-width; centered)
2. Modal closes
3. Inline tooltip behaviour returns

Screen / Section: Fleet Pulse: Status Column Spec + Fleet Pulse: Health Score Tooltip Spec
Requirement Summary: All 6 sub-signal rows must fit inside the default tooltip viewport without internal scrolling. Visual sanity check.
Title: Verify all 6 sub-signal rows are visible inside the Health Score tooltip without internal scrolling
Preconditions: • Browser viewport ≥ 1024 px wide; default zoom 100%

2. Count visible rows

Expected Result:

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §4 Forecast display
Requirement Summary: Forecast must display as relative days (e.g. '47 days') NOT a calendar date. Avoids timezone confusion across the fleet.
Title: Verify forecast is displayed as relative days (e.g. '47 days') not as a calendar date
Preconditions: • Vehicle with forecast=47 d
Steps (Action):
1. Open the row
2. Inspect DOM text
Expected Result:
1. Forecast renders as '47 days'
2. No date format (YYYY-MM-DD or similar) anywhere in the forecast cell

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.2 CRANK Signal (WeatherAPI)
Requirement Summary: NEW v4 coverage: when the vehicle has no GPS fix at the time of a crank event; WeatherAPI cannot resolve ambient temperature. The pipeline must fall back to the last-known fix within 7 days. Review §4.10 flagged this gap.
Title: Verify CRANK temperature compensation falls back to the most recent GPS fix within 7 days when current fix is missing
Preconditions:
• Vehicle has a crank event with no concurrent GPS fix
• Last known GPS fix was 3 days ago at a location whose WeatherAPI temp = 18 °C
Steps (Action):
1. Run CRANK pipeline on the event
2. Inspect ambient_temp_source
3. Inspect ambient_temp_c
4. Inspect compensated_crank_dip
Expected Result:
1. Pipeline runs
2. 'last_known_fix_within_7d'
3. 18 °C
4. Computed using 18 °C; not 25 °C default
Notes: New in v4 — closes review §4.10 gap (no GPS scenario).

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.2 CRANK Signal (WeatherAPI)
Requirement Summary: NEW v4 coverage: when no GPS fix exists in the last 7 days either; the pipeline must fall back to the 25 °C baseline (zero adjustment) and flag the event. Review §4.10 flagged this gap.
Title: Verify CRANK temperature compensation falls back to 25 °C baseline when no GPS fix exists within the last 7 days
Preconditions: • No GPS fix in the last 7 days for the vehicle
Steps (Action):
1. Run CRANK pipeline on a crank event in this state
2. Inspect ambient_temp_source
3. Inspect ambient_temp_c
4. Inspect compensation factor
5. Inspect forecast_audit.warnings
Expected Result:
1. Pipeline runs
2. 'baseline_default'
3. 25 °C
4. 0.025 × (25 − 25) = 0 (no adjustment)
5. Contains 'temp_fallback_baseline'
Notes: New in v4 — closes review §4.10 gap (no fix within 7 d).
